===Walkthrough===
----
{{Instance}}
[[File:Culinaromancer unfrozen.png|thumb|right|The Culinaromancer is unfrozen, and escapes back to his dimension!]]
After you have freed all the council members, you will see a [[cutscene]], where [[Aris]] restarts time by saying "Tempus procedit!" The Culinaromancer will then retreat through the portal back to his own dimension, where he'll be ready to cause more trouble. Aris then says that you too must go through the portal to finish him off.

When you're in the strange dimension, you'll find the Culinaromancer, a few white portals that lead back to [[Lumbridge]], and one of your previous quest bosses. The Culinaromancer is too strong to be defeated until you've defeated the first five bosses. 

'''Note:''' Prayer cannot be used in this dimension.

'''Note:''' You can leave and stock up after defeating each boss without losing your place in the fight.

'''Note:''' Upon trying to enter the portal, Aris will mention that due to dimensional instability, your items will be lost forever if you die. While it's true that no gravestone will appear in the world, '''her claim is false''' because your items can still be retrieved normally from Death's Domain.
====Agrith-Na-Na====
[[Agrith-Na-Na]] is [[Agrith Naar]] from the [[Shadow of the Storm]] quest, but he is now made of bananas and is level 146. He uses [[melee]] up close and [[Fire Blast]] at a distance. Agrith-Na-Na has decent magic attack and defence, so use your best melee or ranged setup. 

====Flambeed====
[[Flambeed]] is [[Fareed]] from [[Desert Treasure I]]. He's level 149 now. You need [[ice gloves]] to wield a weapon. If you attack him without ice gloves, your weapon will return to your inventory. He's harder to defeat without [[prayer]], but you should be able to kill him with plenty of high healing food. You can [[snare]] him, run away, and then mage him so you only use little to no food. He drops [[cake]]s.

====Karamel====
[[Karamel]] is [[Kamil]] from the [[Desert Treasure I]] quest. Fight her up close with a spell that has a lower level requirement than your actual Magic level, preferably a fire spell to take advantage of her elemental weakness. You should not be using your highest level spell, as Karamel will likely lower your magic level to the point that you are no longer able to cast it. Bring [[stat restore potion]]s and lots of food. Even with lowering your stats, Karamel is incredibly inaccurate. A good strategy is to use a [[Bolt spells|Bolt Spell]] with [[Chaos gauntlets]]. As usual, [[Melee]] doesn't work on her very well. Unlike Kamil, Karamel uses ranged attacks at melee range. Since her ranged attacks are not very strong, a good tactic is to stand right next to her so she uses ranged, which reduces how frequently she uses her ice attacks. Manually casting spells is beneficial due to her freezing effects stopping your auto-attacks. She drops a [[choc-ice]].

Note: Even though Kamil is immune to [[Iban Blast]], Karamel is not. Iban Blast can be used very effectively.

====Dessourt====
[[Dessourt]] is [[Dessous]] from [[Desert Treasure I]]. [[Melee]] doesn't work very well on him, however he is less resistant to [[stab weapons]]. [[Magic]] such as [[Iban Blast]] works well. [[Ranged]] also works really well on him. Bring lots of food, and then, defeat him as quickly as possible. He drops [[chocolate dust]].

====Gelatinnoth Mother====
[[Gelatinnoth Mother]] is the [[Dagannoth Mother]] from the [[Horror from the Deep]] quest, with that in mind you will need to bring Water, Air, Earth, and Fire runes in order to attack her during her weakness. She uses a ranged attack that rolls two hits per attack, one magic based and one ranged based. Each hit is capable of hitting for 20 for a possible max hit of 40 per attack. Players who have 80 or less health can die easily with a bit of bad luck and slow eating. The fight itself is fairly easy, although a bit long. She changes colour depending on her weakness. She has a low [[Magic]] [[Defence]] but high Melee Defence, making it best to not bother with Melee and just focus on maging and ranging. A great strategy is to mage her while wearing a mix of the best [[dragonhide armour]] available, a shield such as a [[dragonfire shield]], [[granite shield]], or [[toktz-ket-xil]], and melee helmet such as [[Barrows equipment|Barrows]]. Otherwise, she will rip through [[Mystic robes]] and other magic robes with her ranged attacks. This would give the player a balanced Defence against both Ranged and Magic Defence without sacrificing too much Magic attack bonus.

====Culinaromancer====
After you've defeated the [[Gelatinnoth Mother]], the [[Culinaromancer]] himself will attack you. He's level 75, hits high (can hit rapid 25s with magic), but has practically no [[Defence]]. He uses [[Magic]], but tries to get up close to attack as well. Kill him with any attack style and bring food. It is recommended to use armour that maximizes [[max hit]] (and to a lesser extent [[crush]] defence), as his magic attack has a flat 1-in-3 hit rate, so magic defence is irrelevant.

If you happen to defeat the Culinaromancer with a full inventory, the reward lamp will drop at your feet when you return to the Lumbridge castle. 

A cake will drop to the ground, but you are unable to pick it up. It is mostly symbolic.
